triggerText: Steering committee 14 Apr: ICT scaled the rollout to all 339 partner schools — we no longer need to sample within Islamabad. Implementation partner (Taleemabad) added a third arm (traditional human coaching only, no DC) to satisfy a control condition the funder asked for. Endpoint shifts from take-up/retention to FICO score improvement (the partner's own pedagogical rubric). Coach-to-teacher ratio is fixed at ~10:1 and we should preserve coaching relationships within the sampling structure. Need IRR between DC scoring and human coach scoring — make this part of the design, not a separate exercise. Baseline April, endline May.
summary: Major redesign in response to steering-committee meeting (14 Apr 2026). Two-arm A/B at 200 teachers replaced by a three-arm design across all 339 partner schools (~3,390 teachers), with a new control arm (traditional coaching only). Primary outcome shifts from take-up/retention to FICO score improvement. IRR between DC and human scoring is now embedded in Group A's observation plan rather than a separate validation exercise. Power, measurement, and analysis stages all updated accordingly.

stages.content:
Bundling human coaching with the Digital Coach (DC) produces faster and larger improvement in classroom practice (FICO scores) than DC alone, and both DC-inclusive arms outperform traditional in-person coaching alone — under field conditions where DC is delivered to all teachers in the arm and human observation is conducted on a measurement subsample.

Mechanism (unchanged from v1): DC delivers structured, frequent FICO-aligned feedback. Human coaches add accountability and translation of DC feedback into in-classroom changes. The control arm (traditional coaching only) tests the status-quo counterfactual the funder requires.

**Primary**

1. Does teachers' FICO performance improve over a one-month period of DC use, and does the magnitude of improvement differ between DC + human support (Group A) and DC alone (Group B)?
2. How do improvement trajectories for DC-supported teachers (Groups A and B) compare to those receiving traditional coaching only (Group C)?

**Secondary**

3. What is the minimum number of recordings needed before a measurable improvement in FICO scores is observed?
4. To what extent does teacher engagement (recording frequency) mediate the relationship between DC access and FICO improvement?
5. How closely do DC scores align with human-observer scores across FICO dimensions, and does alignment vary by subject, score level, or point in time? (IRR question.)

**Three-arm design**, school-level assignment across all 339 partner schools (no longer a 25-school sample within Islamabad). Coaching relationships are preserved within the sampling structure: each coach handles ~10 teachers, ≈4.52 schools per coach, 8 school visits per coach in a fortnightly cycle.

| Group | Human coaching | Digital coach | FICO (Human) | FICO (Digital) | Schools | Teachers (treated) | Teachers observed (human) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **A — DC + Human** | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes — DC scores teacher recordings | 113 | ~1,130 | ~500 |
| **B — DC only** | No | Yes | No | Yes — DC scores teacher recordings | 113 | ~1,130 | — |
| **C — Traditional (control)** | Yes | No (teachers don't access DC; coaches submit recordings to DC for parallel scoring) | Yes | Yes — coach recordings scored by DC | 113 | ~1,130 | ~500 |

**Selection within school.** Focus on Foundational Literacy and Numeracy (FLN) and science. Each teacher is observed/recorded across at least two FLN subjects.

**IRR is built into Group A.** A subset of Group A observations are scheduled as **paired observations** — the human coach scores a lesson that is also being recorded and scored by DC, scored independently. No separate IRR sample.

**Design questions still open.** Confirm grade(s) covered; confirm sampled schools have enough eligible FLN/science teachers per coach to support the 10:1 ratio.

**The change in scope changes the power story entirely.** v1 was borderline-powered with 100 per arm. v2 has roughly 500 observed teachers per arm in Groups A and C (1,000 human observations each over 4 weeks, ≈2 per teacher) and ~1,130 DC-using teachers per arm in Groups A and B.

**Unit of analysis.** Teacher, with cluster adjustment for school. ICC for FICO ≈ 0.10 (assumed, to be validated from baseline).

**Design effect.** With ~10 teachers per school, 1 + (10 − 1) × 0.10 = **1.90**.

**Effective sample per arm (FICO comparisons, human-observed subsample).** 500 / 1.90 ≈ **263 per arm**. With α = 0.05 two-sided and 80% power, **MDE on standardised FICO change ≈ 0.24 SD** for pairwise comparisons — well below the 0.30–0.40 SD effects typical of coaching-style interventions. The study is now **adequately powered** for all three pairwise comparisons.

**Engagement-mediation analysis (RQ4).** Uses the full ~1,130 per arm DC-using sample, so much higher precision.

**To recompute / validate before launch:** ICC from baseline FICO data; whether observation timing (~2 per teacher) supports a within-teacher trajectory model rather than just baseline-vs-endline.

**Timeline.** Baseline April 2026, endline May 2026.

**Primary outcome.** Change in FICO score from first to last observation/recording within the study window.

**Assessment tools.**

- Groups A and B: scored by the DC using a FICO-aligned rubric.
- Group C: in-person observation by human coaches using the same FICO framework. Coaches additionally submit lesson recordings for parallel DC scoring (FICO v.3 Digital alongside FICO v.3 Human).
- In Group A, a subset of lessons are also scored by human observers to support **IRR calibration** (see below).

**Comparability.** Because Groups A/B and C use different *primary* scoring modalities (AI vs human), absolute scores are not compared directly across groups. Analysis focuses on **improvement rates** (magnitude of change) and **improvement trajectories** (patterns over time).

**Human observation allocation.** ~2,000 human coach observations total: ~1,000 in Group A (treatment delivery + measurement + IRR) and ~1,000 in Group C (primary measurement + parallel DC scoring). No human observations in Group B.

**IRR — paired-observation design.** A paired observation = human coach scores a lesson that is also recorded and scored by DC. Both scores produced independently (coach blind to DC, DC not adjusted to coach). All IRR pairs drawn from the Group A pool. IRR estimated overall and disaggregated by FICO dimension, subject, score range, and study phase. Systematic biases reported and fed back into DC model refinement.

Each pairwise comparison maps to a distinct policy question.

- **Group A vs Group B** — value of human support layered on DC. Tests whether DC needs human scaffolding or stands alone. Addresses **RQ1**.
- **Group A vs Group C** — does DC + human coaching beat traditional coaching alone? Addresses **RQ2**.
- **Group B vs Group C** — does passive DC access (no enforcement) beat traditional coaching? Addresses **RQ2**.
- **IRR analysis** — agreement between DC and human scoring overall and by dimension/subject/score range/phase. Addresses **RQ5**.

**Models.** Teacher-level OLS on FICO change with cluster-robust SEs at school. Include strata (urban/rural), grade, baseline FICO, and coach fixed effects where the sampling structure allows.

**Trajectory analysis (RQ3, RQ4).** Mixed-effects model with teacher random intercepts, fixed effect of recording number / week, and an interaction with arm. Use the trajectory to identify the recordings-count threshold at which improvement becomes detectable.

**Pre-specification.** Lock the 4 primary comparisons and the trajectory model before unblinding endline data. Multiple-comparison adjustment: Holm across the 3 pairwise FICO comparisons; IRR reported descriptively.

**Robustness.** (i) Re-estimate primary comparisons restricting to teachers with ≥3 DC recordings. (ii) IPW for differential attrition between baseline and endline. (iii) Sensitivity to ICC mis-specification.
_To be populated as observations land. Plan: descriptive baseline tables by 1 May; first interim look at trajectories ~mid-May; primary comparisons after endline closes._

_To be filled in at endline. Anchor questions to revisit: was the IRR-embedded-in-Group-A design operationally workable, or did paired observations introduce scheduling drag? Did the 10:1 coach ratio hold in practice? Did Group B teachers self-initiate enough to make the comparison meaningful?_
